description: REVIEW REQUIRED. LLM-extracted claims and LLM-PROPOSED relationships. Inspect and approve the relationships before running the engine. Reliability is from document metadata, not LLM-judged.

claims.claim_text: Over geological timescales, the continents are not fixed.
The continents move slowly across the surface of the Earth.
The landmasses we see today drifted apart from a single ancestral supercontinent.
Plate tectonics provides the mechanism for continental movement.
Rigid plates carry the continents on the slowly convecting mantle beneath them.
The continents are permanent and fixed in their positions.
The apparent fit between facing coastlines is mere coincidence.
The presence of identical fossils on widely separated continents is explained by ancient land bridges that later sank beneath the oceans.
There is no mechanism by which continents could move.
Several independent lines of evidence support the movement of continents.
The coastlines of facing continents fit together like puzzle pieces.
Identical fossil species appear on continents now separated by oceans.
Matching rock strata line up across ocean margins.
Matching mountain belts line up across ocean margins.
Mid-ocean ridges show symmetric magnetic striping consistent with seafloor spreading.
Most peptic ulcers are caused by infection with the bacterium Helicobacter pylori.
claims.source: Marshall & Warren line of work, peer-reviewed (Nobel 2005)

claims.claim_text: The standard treatment for peptic ulcers is a course of antibiotics.
claims.source: Marshall & Warren line of work, peer-reviewed (Nobel 2005)

claims.claim_text: Antibiotic treatment eradicates the Helicobacter pylori infection and allows the ulcer to heal.
claims.source: Marshall & Warren line of work, peer-reviewed (Nobel 2005)

claims.claim_text: Acid-suppressing drugs support healing of peptic ulcers but do not address the root cause.
claims.source: Marshall & Warren line of work, peer-reviewed (Nobel 2005)

claims.claim_text: Peptic ulcers are caused by psychological stress.
Peptic ulcers are caused by eating spicy food.
The stomach is too acidic an environment for any bacteria to survive.
Bacterial infection plays no role in peptic ulcers.
Peptic ulcers are best managed with rest and stress reduction.
Peptic ulcers are best managed with a bland diet that avoids irritating foods.
Laboratory and clinical findings support a bacterial cause for peptic ulcers.
Curved bacteria are visible on biopsy of inflamed stomach lining.
The organism associated with peptic ulcers has been cultured from gastric tissue.
A researcher deliberately ingested the organism and developed gastritis.
Antibiotic treatment cleared both the bacterial infection and the ulcer together.

relationships.rationale: The slow movement of continents across Earth's surface directly illustrates that they are not fixed over geological timescales.
Landmasses drifting apart from a single supercontinent confirms that continents are not fixed in their positions.
Plate tectonics provides the physical mechanism that explains how continents move slowly across Earth's surface.
The description of rigid plates carried by convecting mantle elaborates on and supports the plate tectonics mechanism.
Mantle convection carrying rigid plates directly explains the slow movement of continents.
Multiple independent lines of evidence reinforce the conclusion that continents are not fixed.
Independent lines of evidence collectively support the claim that continents move across Earth's surface.
A puzzle-piece fit of coastlines is a systematic match, directly contradicting the claim that it is mere coincidence.
The coastline fit is one of the independent lines of evidence supporting continental movement.
Identical fossils on separated continents constitute one of the independent lines of evidence for continental drift.
Identical fossil species on now-separated continents supports the idea that those landmasses were once joined in a single supercontinent.
Matching rock strata across ocean margins is one of the independent lines of evidence for continental movement.
Matching mountain belts across ocean margins constitute another independent line of evidence for continental drift.
Symmetric magnetic striping at mid-ocean ridges is an independent line of evidence consistent with seafloor spreading and continental movement.
Magnetic striping is direct observational evidence for seafloor spreading, a key component of plate tectonics.
Plate tectonics provides exactly the mechanism that the outdated claim asserts does not exist.
Mantle convection driving rigid plates is a concrete mechanism for continental movement, refuting the claim that no such mechanism exists.
Continents being non-fixed over geological timescales directly contradicts the claim that they are permanent and fixed.
Continents moving slowly across Earth's surface is irreconcilable with the claim that they are fixed in position.
Multiple independent lines of evidence for movement directly contradict the assertion that continents are fixed.
Continental drift explains identical fossils on separated continents without requiring land bridges that later sank, making the land-bridge explanation unnecessary.
A bacterial cause for most peptic ulcers contradicts the claim that they are caused by psychological stress.
A bacterial cause for most peptic ulcers contradicts the claim that they are caused by spicy food.
H. pylori being the primary cause of peptic ulcers directly contradicts the claim that bacterial infection plays no role.
Antibiotic treatment as the standard care contradicts the claim that rest and stress reduction are the best management.
Antibiotic treatment as the standard care contradicts the claim that a bland diet is the best management.
The fact that antibiotics eradicate H. pylori and allow healing explains why antibiotic treatment is the standard approach.
Antibiotic eradication of H. pylori leading to ulcer healing reinforces that H. pylori infection is the cause of most ulcers.
Acid suppressants not addressing the root cause supports the primacy of antibiotic treatment to eradicate H. pylori.
The claim that no bacteria can survive in the stomach contradicts the finding that H. pylori infects and causes ulcers there.
Believing the stomach is too acidic for bacteria supports the conclusion that bacterial infection plays no role in ulcers.
Laboratory and clinical findings supporting a bacterial cause directly reinforce the claim that H. pylori causes most peptic ulcers.
Visible curved bacteria on biopsy of inflamed stomach lining is one of the laboratory findings supporting a bacterial cause.
Bacteria visibly present in gastric tissue directly contradicts the claim that the stomach is too acidic for bacteria to survive.
Successful culturing of the organism from gastric tissue is a key laboratory finding supporting a bacterial cause.
Culturing bacteria from gastric tissue proves bacteria can survive in the stomach, contradicting the claim they cannot.
A researcher deliberately ingesting the organism and developing gastritis is a clinical demonstration of the bacterial cause.
Deliberate ingestion causing gastritis proves bacteria do play a causal role, directly contradicting the claim they play no role.
Antibiotic treatment clearing both infection and ulcer simultaneously is a clinical finding that supports the bacterial cause.
Observing that antibiotics clear the infection and heal the ulcer together directly supports the mechanism described in this claim.
Antibiotics resolving ulcers by clearing bacteria proves that bacterial infection does play a role in peptic ulcers.
Antibiotic treatment resolving ulcers contradicts the claim that rest and stress reduction are the best management.
Antibiotic treatment resolving ulcers contradicts the claim that a bland diet is the best management.
